LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, APR-30-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT LORETTA JOHNSON'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. LORETTA JOHNSON'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR LORETTA JOHNSON IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

EXPEDITED PETITION TO APPROVE COMPROMISE OF DISPUTED CLAIM
Off Calendar Jun-16-2023 Continued To Jul-05-23 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 505

EXPEDITED PETITION TO APPROVE COMPROMISE OF DISPUTED CLAIM
Off Calendar Jul-05-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for Plaintiff's motion for compromise of claim of Minor, Josiah Johnson.

Dept. 505; Judge Gail Dekreon; Clerk: N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 2:09:16 p.m. and concluding at 2:15:36 p.m.

Guardian ad Litem Adrienna Johnson appearing represented by Ross McKissick, Esq., is placed under oath and testifies.
Defendant Loretta Johnson not appearing.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents brief recitation of case.
Guardian ad Litem is examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.
The Court finds the settlement is reasonable and the Guardian ad Litem confirms it is in the best interest of the claimant.

The court hereby APPROVES Plaintiff's Motion for Compromise of Claim for the settlement amount of $15,000.00minus the following:

1. $1,500.00 for Attorney's Fees
2. $5,130.35 for Medical Expenses
3. $2,479.82 for Costs

Balance of settlement in the sum of $5,889.83 shall be deposited in a blocked account at Redwood Credit Union at 1390 Market Street, San Francisco, CA 94102

The Plaintiff is authorized and directed to execute all documents reasonably necessary to carry out the terms of the settlement.

Proposed Orders Approving Compromise of Claim or Action or Disposition of Proceeds of Judgment for Minor or Person with a Disability and to Deposit Funds in Blocked Account are signed and filed this date.

Court orders matter continued to September 15, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for progress report on the deposit of funds in a blocked account. (D505)

MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for Plaintiff's motion for compromise of claim of Minor, Josiah Johnson.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:00:35 a.m. and concluding at 10:20:15 a.m.

Plaintiff Josiah Johnson appearing represented by Ross McKissick, Esq. and is placed under oath via Zoom video conference.
Guardian ad Litem Adrienna Johnson appearing represented by Ross McKissick, Esq. and is placed under oath and testified via Zoom video conference.

Defendant Loretta Johnson not appearing.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents brief recitation of case.
Guardian ad Litem is examined by the Court.

Guardian ad Litem dose not believe the settlement amount is in the best interest of the minor. Court orders Guardian ad Litem to investigate and ask more questions in an attempt to negotiate a more reasonable settlement.

Court continues the matter to April 26, 2023 at 9:00 am in Department 505 for further hearing on plaintiff's petition for compromise of claim. (D505)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ROSS MCKISSICK, Esq. of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers, Inc. (Tel: 415-495-0440) for plaintiff Josiah Johnson by his GAL Adrienna Johnson;
Atty. ERIC E. WILLIAMS, Esq. of Carbone, Smith & Koyama (Tel: 510-267-7250) for defendant Loretta Marie Johnson.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LORETTA JOHNSON's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Loretta Johnson's unopposed motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted.

Counsel for Loretta Johnson is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
